games.metaDescription: Social games designed for hanging out in person. The system moderates, so everyone gets to play.
games.lead: Games I designed to play with friends when hanging out in person. Traditionally, someone has to sit out and act as the moderator, which is annoying when there aren't many people. With these games, the system handles the moderation completely, so everyone (including the host) gets to jump in and play.
games.imposter.cardDescription: No one sits out as moderator! The system runs the game. One word, one imposter. Describe, debate, and vote.
imposter.title: Word Imposter
imposter.subtitle: Gather friends, create a room, and play on your phones. One of you is the Imposter, but no one has to moderate. No app, no sign-up.
imposter.modeLead: The system acts as moderator. Everyone gets the word except the Imposter. Describe, discuss, vote.
imposter.steps.create.title: Create a room
imposter.steps.create.body: Copy the code and send it to your crew.
imposter.steps.join.title: Join
imposter.steps.join.body: Pick a name and sit tight in the lobby.
imposter.steps.start.title: Game starts
imposter.steps.start.body: The system puts everyone in. Imposter is picked secretly.
imposter.steps.discuss.title: Discuss
imposter.steps.discuss.body: Don’t say the word. Imposter tries to fit in — catch them.
imposter.steps.voteStart.title: Start voting
imposter.steps.voteStart.body: Vote fires up when clues are done. Change your pick until the reveal.
imposter.steps.reveal.title: Reveal & next round
imposter.steps.reveal.body: System reveals who got caught. Jump into the next round.
imposter.tips.title: Tips
imposter.tips.1: Everyone opens on their own device. No peeking at someone else’s screen.
imposter.tips.2: Keep your clue vague: shape, vibe, feeling — don’t make it too obvious.
imposter.tips.3: Don't forget to pick the language in the room — clues follow that language.
imposter.tips.4: One clue per person, then vote. Game flows way better that way.
imposter.roomMetaDescription: Get in the lobby and grab your hidden role. No referee needed—the system runs everything.
imposter.roomLead: Send the code to your friends, gather in the lobby, and start playing (without needing a moderator).
imposter.placeholder.name: Timurhan

imposter.waitingForRole: Your role is on the way…
imposter.voting.title: Voting
imposter.voting.lead: Pick your suspect. Switch anytime before the reveal.

imposter.results.imposterGuessed.desc: The Imposter stole the win by guessing the secret word.
imposter.results.crewWinsDesc: The crew caught the right person — :name was the imposter!
imposter.results.imposterWinsDesc: :name blended in perfectly — the crew missed.
imposter.results.imposterWinsGuessedDesc: The Imposter guessed the secret word and took the win.
imposter.modal.confirmGuessed.title: Imposter Guessed the Word?
imposter.modal.confirmGuessed.desc: Are you sure the Imposter successfully guessed the secret word? This will end the game immediately and give the win to the Imposter.
imposter.voting.yourTurn: Your turn! Select who you think is the imposter.
imposter.voting.allVoted: Everyone has voted. Waiting for results.
imposter.joinThisRoomDesc: Drop a name to show up in the lobby.
imposter.waitingForStart: Waiting for the game to start…
imposter.waitingForStartDesc: Once the game starts, your role shows up here.
imposter.privateRole: Private — don't show this to anyone.
imposter.noWord: No word for you. Wing it.
imposter.describeWithoutSaying: Describe it — just don't say it.
imposter.keepPrivate: Keep this tab to yourself — role secrecy is the whole point.
imposter.rulesLine1: Gather your friends in person and open a room. The system secretly picks one Imposter—no one knows who it is (not even the host!).
imposter.rulesLine2: If you're Crew, you see the secret word. Take turns giving a vague clue. If you're the Imposter, just play along and try to blend in.
imposter.rulesLine3: Once everyone gives their clue, debate and cast your votes on your screens. The system handles the reveal, and you can jump to the next round with one click.
imposter.rulesTip: Use your own phone and don't show your screen to anyone.
games.vampire.cardDescription: The system moderates so everyone plays. Night falls, vampires hunt. Dawn breaks, villagers vote.
vampire.title: Vampire Village
vampire.subtitle: Night: vampires hunt. Day: villagers vote. The system moderates, so the host plays too. No app needed.
vampire.roomMetaDescription: Join the lobby and grab your secret role. The system moderates, so the creator plays too.
vampire.roomLead: Share the code with your friends, join the lobby, and let the system run the game.
vampire.placeholder.name: Timurhan
vampire.createRoomDesc: Pick your name. You're the host — you start the rounds (don't worry, you get to play too).
vampire.joinRoomDesc: Enter the code your friend shared.
vampire.joinError: Could not join. Room not found.
vampire.joinThisRoomDesc: Enter your name to show in the lobby.
vampire.waitingForStartDesc: When the game starts, your secret role appears here.
vampire.roomNotFoundDesc: That code doesn't exist or expired. Go back and create a new room.
vampire.gameAlreadyStartedDesc: You can't join this round. Wait for the next one.
vampire.rulesLine1: The biggest difference from the traditional game: Since the system acts as the moderator, the creator of the room also gets a secret role and plays like everyone else.
vampire.rulesLine2: Night: Everyone closes their eyes. The system calls out the roles to wake up one by one (Vampires, Doctor, Detective). You secretly perform your action on your phone.
vampire.rulesLine3: Dawn: The events of the night (who died, who was saved) are automatically summarized and displayed on the screen.
vampire.rulesLine4: Day: Everyone debates. Vote on your screen to eliminate a suspect. The cycle repeats until one faction wins.
vampire.config.title: Role setup
vampire.config.hint: Total roles must equal number of players.
vampire.config.errorMinPlayers: Need at least 4 players to start.
vampire.config.errorMismatch: Role count (:required) doesn't match player count (:players). Adjust the config.
vampire.night.title: Night :number
vampire.night.desc: Eyes closed. The system guides each phase on screen.
vampire.night.vampireVoteDesc: Vote together. Majority wins — tie means no kill.
vampire.night.doctorProtectDesc: Choose who to protect tonight. Your choice stays hidden.
vampire.night.detectiveQueryDesc: Choose one player. You'll learn if they're a vampire.
vampire.night.waitingDesc: Keep your eyes shut. It's not your turn — stay quiet.
vampire.dawn.title: Dawn
vampire.hunter.title: Hunter's Last Shot
vampire.hunter.subtitle: The hunter was killed — but gets one last shot.
vampire.hunter.desc: Hunter: choose your target before the day begins.
vampire.hunter.waiting: Waiting for the Hunter to act…
vampire.day.title: Daytime — Discuss
vampire.day.desc: Debate, accuse, and convince. Voting starts when you're ready.

vampire.voting.lead: Vote once. Change anytime before reveal.
vampire.voting.eliminatedCannotVote: You've been eliminated and cannot vote.
